Function Documentation

size_t array_get_nref_size ( array_t *  array  ) 

Gets the size (in bytes) of the non-reference area of an array

Parameters:
array A pointer to the array header
Returns:
The size (in bytes) of the array's non-reference area

Gets the number of references in an array

array A pointer to an array header

void arraycopy_ref ( array_t *  src,
int32_t  src_offset,
array_t *  dest,
int32_t  dest_offset,
int32_t  length 
)

C implementation of java.lang.System.arraycopy() for reference arrays. If an element is found not to be compatible with the destination array an ArrayStoreException is thrown

Parameters:
src A pointer to the source array
src_offset The offset in the source array
dest A pointer to the destination array
dest_offset The offset in the destination array
length The number of elements to copy

Definition at line 135 of file array.c.

References array_ref_get_data(), bcl_is_assignable(), class_t::elem_class, gc_new_array_ref(), array_t::header, header_get_class(), and KNI_ThrowNew().

Referenced by java_lang_System_arraycopy().

Variable Documentation

uint8_t array_elem_sizes[]
Initial value:
 {
    1, 
    2, 
    4, 
    8, 
    1, 
    2, 
    4, 
    8  
}

This array holds the size in bytes of the basic array element types. T_BOOLEAN is a special case as it set as having an element size of 1 byte per element when in fact it uses 1 bit per element

Definition at line 55 of file array.c.

Referenced by array_elem_size().

    T_SHORT,   
    T_INT,     
    T_FLOAT,

This array holds an array_type_t value for each primitive_type_t element which can be used in a non-reference array
